design_philosophy: Enterprise-grade, premium product inspired by Vercel, Linear, and OpenAI. Technical precision meets minimalist luxury. Avoid generic AI slop gradients. Utilize tight typography, massive negative space, and micro-interactions.

headings.description: Used for all H1-H4. Delivers a sharp, geometric, and highly technical look.

body.description: Replaces Inter/Roboto. Highly legible, modern grotesque for clean reading.

monospace.description: Essential for the DevSecOps/Cybersecurity vibe. Used for terminal, code blocks, labels, and stats.

gradients.spotlight: radial-gradient(600px circle at var(--mouse-x) var(--mouse-y), rgba(255,255,255,0.06), transparent 40%)

visual_enhancers.noise_texture: Apply a 2-4% opacity SVG noise overlay across the background to remove digital flatness.
visual_enhancers.borders: 1px solid border_subtle. Avoid heavy shadows; rely on border contrast.
visual_enhancers.blur: backdrop-blur-xl (16px to 24px) for sticky navs and modal overlays.

buttons.primary: Sharp edges (rounded-none or max rounded-sm), border 1px solid white/20, hover bg-white/10, text-white. Magnetic hover effect.
buttons.secondary: Ghost style, text-secondary hover:text-white transition-colors duration-300.

cards.base: Glassmorphism, bg-white/[0.02], border border-white/5, backdrop-blur-md, internal padding p-6 or p-8.
cards.hover: Tilt effect (Framer Motion), subtle border illumination matching mouse position (mouse spotlight).

badges.base: Pill-shaped (rounded-full), bg-white/5, border border-white/10, font-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-widest px-3 py-1.

terminal.container: bg-[#0A0A0A], border border-[#333], rounded-lg shadow-2xl overflow-hidden
terminal.header: h-10 bg-[#1A1A1A] border-b border-[#333] flex items-center px-4 space-x-2 (mac-like traffic lights in grays)

grids_layouts.spacing_system: Use generous spacing. Tailwind p-8 to p-24 for section margins. Elements must breathe.
grids_layouts.bento_grid: For Cyber/DevSecOps skills. grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-4 lg:grid-cols-6 gap-4 or gap-6. Mix tall (row-span-2) and wide (col-span-2) cards.

motion_timing_tokens.parallax: Tied to scroll progress via Framer Motion useScroll()

hero.layout: Min-h-screen, center aligned structurally but asymmetrical in element placement.
hero.elements: Dynamic role rotation (typing or flip), metallic gradient text for name, availability badge (pulsing green dot), primary CTA, aurora background glow.
hero.tech_ticker: react-fast-marquee at bottom of hero. Monospace, uppercase, text-white/30, paused on hover.

projects.layout: Grid of premium cards. 1 or 2 columns max for high detail.
projects.elements: Project Cards feature subtle tech image abstract, tags (React, AWS, etc.), and 'View Case Study' button. On click -> detailed page with Architecture, Implementation, and DevSecOps Pipeline visualization.

global_network.layout: Full width, h-[60vh] to h-[80vh], deep black background.
global_network.elements: Three.js Globe (react-globe.gl) with point clouds, glowing cyan routes. Draggable. Represents global deployments/edge networks.

terminal_section.elements: Interactive. Types 'help' automatically, user can type 'whoami', 'skills', 'clear'. Blinking cursor.

devsecops_pipeline.layout: Horizontal scrolling or staggered vertical nodes.
devsecops_pipeline.elements: Animated nodes (Code -> Build -> Test -> Sec Scan -> Deploy) with active states moving through the pipeline.

accessibility.focus: All interactive elements must have focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-accent_primary outline-none.
accessibility.aria: Use aria-hidden on decorative animations. Provide aria-labels for terminal inputs and 3D canvas.
accessibility.motion: Respect prefers-reduced-motion CSS media query by disabling parallax and fast tilts.

recommended.name: react-globe.gl
recommended.purpose: Interactive 3D network globe for the Global Network section.

recommended.name: framer-motion
recommended.purpose: Core animation engine for tilt cards, scroll reveals, and staggering.

recommended.name: @studio-freight/react-lenis
recommended.purpose: Momentum smooth scrolling for premium feel.

image_urls.description: Dark, moody telecom/cloud tower. Good for abstract infrastructure representation.
Detailed view of dark-themed code, excellent for DevSecOps/AppSec project thumbnails.
Dark server rack with blinking green lights. Great for DevOps/Infrastructure backgrounds.

instructions_to_main_agent: You are building a premium product, not a template. Do not center everything. Use structural asymmetry.
Add 'data-testid' to all interactive elements for testing.
Implement the Lenis smooth scroll wrapping the entire app layout.
Ensure the terminal component handles input state correctly and provides mock outputs for commands.
Do not use generic Shadcn cards without modifying them to fit the glass/minimalist border requirements.
Use lucide-react for UI icons, or optionally Phosphor icons for a technical feel.
Ensure 'react-globe.gl' runs inside a wrapper that prevents hydration errors (e.g., dynamically load if Next.js, or standard useEffect mounting for plain React).
Do NOT use pure transparent backgrounds for sticky headers; use backdrop-blur with a semi-transparent base.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Avoid generic, 'on distribution' AI outputs. Craft distinct frontends.
Dark colors must not be used as generic gradients. Use solid dark colors with noise or hyper-specific radial glows (spotlights).
Create depth via z-index hierarchy and layered backdrops (blur).
Never use transition: all. Target specific properties (opacity, transform, background-color).
Never center-align the main App container in CSS (.App { text-align: center; } is strictly forbidden).
Do not use emojis (🤖🧠) for icons. Use SVG icon libraries.
Apply 2-3x more spacing than typical templates (p-8, p-12, p-24).
All toasts must use the 'sonner' library.
Component Reuse: Prioritize existing components in src/components/ui.
Test Attributes: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid attribute in kebab-case format based on role (e.g., 'terminal-input', 'submit-contact-button').
